verdict_reasoning: IZUMO1 is a type I transmembrane glycoprotein with a structured extracellular domain (Izumo + Ig-like domains) stably anchored to the plasma membrane via its own transmembrane domain. The protein is expressed sperm-specifically and is surface-accessible post-acrosome reaction, qualifying as a classic gamete adhesion molecule and cell-surface fusogen. While the protein reaches the outer leaflet via its own TM anchor mechanism (meeting the cardinal recruitment test), surface presentation is restricted to a specific cell state (acrosome-reacted sperm), warranting a contextual rather than unconditional yes verdict.
